---
title: "The 4/20 Effect: Fatal Vehicle Crashes and the Annual Cannabis Holiday"
author: "Daniel Dawit, Pranav Kannan, Sid Mehrok, Satvik Kannekanti"
date: today
format:
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    geometry: margin=1in
    fontsize: 12pt
    number-sections: true
    toc: false
    fig-pos: "H"
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## Data

### Sources

This analysis uses two datasets from the TidyTuesday 2025-04-22 release, both derived from FARS:

- **`daily_accidents_420.csv`** (9,170 observations): One row per calendar day, with a binary indicator marking April 20th (`cannabis_holiday`) and `fatalities_count` recording total daily fatal crashes. This dataset spans 1992–2016.

- **`daily_accidents_420_time.csv`** (23,086 observations): Two rows per calendar day: one for the 4:20pm–11:59pm window (`evening_window = TRUE`) and one for the remainder of the day (`evening_window = FALSE`) with `fatalities_count` recording crashes in each window. This dataset also spans 1992–2016.
